clusters.title: EU lawmaker investigating surveillance hacked by Israeli spyware, report says

Reported by Al Jazeera, Bloomberg Technology, Wired

items.title: EU lawmaker investigating surveillance hacked by Israeli spyware, report says
items.standfirst: Greek former parliamentarian Stelios Kouloglou targeted with Pegasus software, Citizen Lab analysis finds.
items.content: Greek former parliamentarian Stelios Kouloglou targeted with Pegasus software, Citizen Lab analysis finds.

items.title: Greek Politician Investigating Spyware Had Mobile Phone Hacked
items.standfirst: The mobile phone of a Greek politician was repeatedly hacked by spyware while he was working on a European Parliament investigation into sellers of the surveillance technology, new research has found.
items.content: The mobile phone of a Greek politician was repeatedly hacked by spyware while he was working on a European Parliament investigation into sellers of the surveillance technology, new research has found.

items.title: EU Politicians Investigated Pegasus Spyware. Then It Ended Up on One of Their Phones
items.standfirst: “It is a direct attack on the rule of law,” says one European Parliament member of the new findings from Citizen Lab.
items.content: “It is a direct attack on the rule of law,” says one European Parliament member of the new findings from Citizen Lab.

clusters.title: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February

items.title: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
items.standfirst: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.
items.content: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.

items.title: Middle East live: Khamenei laid in state in Tehran as dignitaries pay tribute ahead of funeral
items.standfirst: The body of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was lying in state in Tehran’s Grand Mosalla religious complex Friday as foreign dignitaries, multi-faith religious leaders and senior officials paid tribute to Iran’s slain leader. Funeral ceremonies and processions will be held for a week across the country. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: The body of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was lying in state in Tehran’s Grand Mosalla religious complex Friday as foreign dignitaries, multi-faith religious leaders and senior officials paid tribute to Iran’s slain leader. Funeral ceremonies and processions will be held for a week across the country. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Trump says U.S. keeping current support levels for NATO would be "ridiculous"
items.standfirst: President Trump says it would be "ridiculous" for the United States to continue its "one sided" relationship with NATO. His remarks came less than a week before a NATO summit in Turkey.
items.content: President Trump says it would be "ridiculous" for the United States to continue its "one sided" relationship with NATO. His remarks came less than a week before a NATO summit in Turkey.

items.title: ‘Ridiculous’ for US to maintain current Nato support, Trump warns ahead of alliance summit
items.standfirst: President says Washington’s relationship with Nato is ‘not reciprocal’ and ‘they were not there for us’ in Iran warDonald Trump has said it is “ridiculous” for the US to continue its “one sided” relationship with Nato, less than a week before a summit of the military alliance in Ankara.Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform that “They were not there for us!!!” and Washington’s relationship with Nato “is not reciprocal”. Continue reading...

items.content: Monaco's prosecutor's office say the suspect is a foreign national and no longer in Monaco.

items.title: Prosecutors identify suspect in Monaco parcel bombing
items.standfirst: Monaco authorities have identified a suspect and issued an international arrest warrant in the investigation into a parcel bombing that seriously wounded a sanctioned Ukraine-born millionaire and two others, prosecutors said Thursday.
items.content: Monaco authorities have identified a suspect and issued an international arrest warrant in the investigation into a parcel bombing that seriously wounded a sanctioned Ukraine-born millionaire and two others, prosecutors said Thursday.

clusters.title: Protests turn violent in Albania over development backed by Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner
clusters.neutral_headline: Violent protests over luxury resort linked to Trump's son-in-law
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Protests turn violent in Albania over development backed by Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner
items.standfirst: Anti-government protests turn violent in Albania as demonstrators call for the prime minister to step down after his government greenlit a plan backed by members of the Trump family to transform protected shorelines into luxury real estate. NBC News' Molly Hunter reports.
items.content: Anti-government protests turn violent in Albania as demonstrators call for the prime minister to step down after his government greenlit a plan backed by members of the Trump family to transform protected shorelines into luxury real estate. NBC News' Molly Hunter reports.

items.title: Violent protests over luxury resort linked to Trump's son-in-law
items.standfirst: Police in Albania have clashed with protesters during an anti-government demonstration linked to a luxury resort connected to Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner.
items.content: Police in Albania have clashed with protesters during an anti-government demonstration linked to a luxury resort connected to Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner.

clusters.title: Mexico scene shows crowds that killed three during World Cup celebration
clusters.neutral_headline: Four people die in mass fan celebrations in Mexico City after World Cup victory
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Mexico scene shows crowds that killed three during World Cup celebration
items.standfirst: Two women and one man died of asphyxiation in downtown Mexico City as thousands of soccer fans celebrated their national team’s World Cup win over Ecuador.
items.content: Two women and one man died of asphyxiation in downtown Mexico City as thousands of soccer fans celebrated their national team’s World Cup win over Ecuador.

items.title: Four people die in mass fan celebrations in Mexico City after World Cup victory
items.standfirst: Three of the victims died from suffocationMore than a million people gathered in Mexico CityFour people died, three ⁠from suffocation, as thousands of fans crowded Mexico City streets during World Cup celebrations, the capital’s health secretariat ⁠said in the early ⁠hours ​of Wednesday.The deaths occurred near the Angel of Independence landmark, where thousands of fans had gathered to ⁠celebrate Mexico’s 2-0 victory over Ecuador in the last 32. Continue reading...
